description: hsv_to_rgb expects hue in [0,360) but 5/7 callers pass [0,1] values after the 4995bbe 'fix' — every caller divides by 360 before calling, then the function divides by 360 again. Effective hue stuck near 0 (always redish). Only AudioSpectrumScene and weather_rendering survived with *360 factor intact
renderWin: hue computed as fmod(hue_offset + (x+y)*20/360, 1.0) giving [0,1], but hsv_to_rgb expects [0,360)
getSnakeColor: hue computed as fmod(segment_index*30/360 + time_factor*50/360, 1.0) giving [0,1], but hsv_to_rgb expects [0,360)
renderMathematicalVisualization: hue = iterations/max_iterations + time*50/360 gives [0,~1], but hsv_to_rgb expects [0,360)
hue = fmod(normalized_x + time*0.1, 1.0) gives [0,1], but hsv_to_rgb expects [0,360)
hue = fmod(smoothed*0.01 + color_shift, 1.0) gives [0,1], but hsv_to_rgb expects [0,360)

handle_providers(req, preset_id, scene_id) — arguments swapped: function signature expects (req, scene_id, preset_id) but caller passes (req, preset_id, scene_id). Causes preset lookup to receive scene_id and scene match to receive preset_id

Old curr_content_stream (MemStreamIO*) deleted at line 340 before old curr_animation (StreamReader, which references the same MemStreamIO) is destroyed at line 345. If StreamReader destructor touches the StreamIO, this is use-after-free
generate_random_string uses static std::mt19937 rng without mutex — data race if /spotify/login route receives concurrent requests (restinio may dispatch to multiple threads)
drawGlowingBorder constructs rgb_matrix::Color(color.r * intensity, ...) where color.r is uint8_t and intensity is float. Narrowing float→uint8_t passed to Color ctor — truncation, no rounding
